permission_systems.description: GitHub OAuth 2.0 authorization scopes that control what actions an OAuth App can perform on behalf of an authenticated user.
permission_systems.applies_to: GitHub repositories, organizations, user accounts, packages, and related resources

notes: GitHub OAuth Apps do not publish per-scope risk ratings. All risk_rating_by_vendor values are UNRATED.
The 'repo' and 'public_repo' scopes are compound scopes bundling multiple distinct operation types; normalization_confidence is below 0.75 — see normalization_notes on each entry.
OAuth scope tokens are always additive grants (allow). The deny concept does not apply in the OAuth 2.0 scope model.
GitHub's scope model is user-delegated: a token can never exceed the permissions of the authenticated user regardless of which scopes are requested.

permissions.name: Public read-only (no scope)
permissions.description: Grants read-only access to public information (including user profile info, repository info, and gists)
permissions.notes: Baseline access level requiring no explicit scope token. Any authenticated request without a scope receives public read-only access.

permissions.name: Full repository access
permissions.description: Grants full access to public and private repositories including read and write access to code, commit statuses, repository invitations, collaborators, deployment statuses, and repository webhooks
normalization_notes: schema_fit 0.25: compound scope bundling code read/write, commit statuses, invitations, collaborators, deployment statuses, and webhooks across all public and private repositories — multiple distinct operation types cannot be accurately represented in a single entry
action_clarity 0.50: description covers the end-state but not the individual sub-operations across 6+ resource types

permissions.name: Repository commit status
permissions.description: Grants read/write access to commit statuses in public and private repositories. This scope is only necessary to grant other users or services access to private repository commit statuses without granting access to the code.

permissions.name: Repository deployment status
permissions.description: Grants access to deployment statuses for public and private repositories. This scope is only necessary to grant other users or services access to deployment statuses, without granting access to the code.
permissions.notes: The verb 'access' in the description is ambiguous between read-only and read/write. Treated as read-only based on the stated purpose (accessing without granting code access).

permissions.name: Public repository access
permissions.description: Limits access to public repositories. That includes read/write access to code, commit statuses, repository projects, collaborators, and deployment statuses for public repositories and organizations. Also required for starring public repositories.
normalization_notes: schema_fit 0.50: bundles read/write code, commit statuses, repository projects, collaborators, and deployment statuses into one scope — distinct operation types across multiple resource categories

permissions.name: Repository invitation
permissions.description: Grants accept/decline abilities for invitations to collaborate on a repository. This scope is only necessary to grant other users or services access to invites without granting access to the code.

permissions.name: Security events
permissions.description: Grants read and write access to security events in the code scanning API. This scope is only necessary to grant other users or services access to security events without granting access to the code.

permissions.name: Repository webhook admin
permissions.description: Grants read, write, ping, and delete access to repository hooks in public or private repositories. The repo and public_repo scopes grant full access to repositories, including repository hooks. Use the admin:repo_hook scope to limit access to only repository hooks.

permissions.name: Repository webhook write
permissions.description: Grants read, write, and ping access to hooks in public or private repositories.

permissions.name: Repository webhook read
permissions.description: Grants read and ping access to hooks in public or private repositories.

permissions.name: Organization admin
permissions.description: Fully manage the organization and its teams, projects, and memberships

permissions.name: Organization write
permissions.description: Read and write access to organization membership, organization projects, and team membership

permissions.name: Organization read
permissions.description: Read-only access to organization membership, organization projects, and team membership

permissions.name: SSH public key admin
permissions.description: Fully manage public keys
permissions.notes: Ability to delete SSH keys is a high-risk capability enabling account lockout or unauthorized access takeover.

permissions.name: SSH public key write
permissions.description: Create, list, and view details for public keys

permissions.name: SSH public key read
permissions.description: List and view details for public keys

permissions.name: Organization webhook admin
permissions.description: Grants read, write, ping, and delete access to organization hooks
permissions.notes: OAuth tokens can only manage organization hooks created by the OAuth App. Personal access tokens can only manage hooks created by the user.

permissions.name: Gist write
permissions.description: Grants write access to gists
permissions.notes: The description states 'write access' but does not clarify whether this includes delete. Treated as write-only (create/update) based on the verb used.

permissions.name: Notifications
permissions.description: Grants read access to a user's notifications, mark as read access to threads, watch and unwatch access to a repository, and read, write, and delete access to thread subscriptions
permissions.notes: Delete access applies only to thread subscriptions (notification preferences), not to notifications themselves. Overall impact is low.

permissions.name: Full user profile
permissions.description: Grants read/write access to profile info only. Note that this scope includes user:email and user:follow.

permissions.name: User profile read
permissions.description: Grants access to read a user's profile data

permissions.name: User email addresses
permissions.description: Grants read access to a user's email addresses
permissions.notes: Exposes both public and private email addresses. Users may have a private email configured that is not shown on their public profile.

permissions.name: User follow
permissions.description: Grants access to follow or unfollow other users

permissions.name: Projects read/write
permissions.description: Grants read/write access to user and organization projects
permissions.notes: Applies to project boards, not repository-level projects.

permissions.name: Projects read
permissions.description: Grants read only access to user and organization projects

permissions.name: Repository delete
permissions.description: Grants access to delete adminable repositories
permissions.notes: Only repositories where the authenticated user has admin rights can be deleted. Irreversible without a GitHub support restore request.

permissions.name: Package publish
permissions.description: Grants access to upload or publish a package in GitHub Packages

permissions.name: Package download
permissions.description: Grants access to download or install packages from GitHub Packages

permissions.name: Package delete
permissions.description: Grants access to delete packages from GitHub Packages

permissions.name: GPG key admin
permissions.description: Fully manage GPG keys
permissions.notes: Ability to delete GPG signing keys can break commit signature verification and may be used to discredit signed commits.

permissions.name: GPG key write
permissions.description: Create, list, and view details for GPG keys

permissions.name: GPG key read
permissions.description: List and view details for GPG keys

permissions.name: Codespace management
permissions.description: Grants the ability to create and manage codespaces. Codespaces can expose a GITHUB_TOKEN which may have a different set of scopes. See the Codespaces security documentation for more details.
permissions.notes: The GITHUB_TOKEN exposed within a codespace environment may carry a broader or different scope set than the token that created the codespace, creating an indirect privilege escalation path.

permissions.name: GitHub Actions workflow write
permissions.description: Grants the ability to add and update GitHub Actions workflow files. Workflow files can be committed without this scope if the same file (with the same path and contents) exists on another branch in the same repository. Workflow files can expose GITHUB_TOKEN which may have a different set of scopes.
permissions.notes: An attacker with this scope can introduce malicious workflow steps that exfiltrate secrets, pivot to cloud infrastructure, or execute arbitrary code in CI runners.

permissions.name: Audit log read
permissions.description: Read audit log data
permissions.notes: Vendor description is terse ('Read audit log data'). Scope provides read access to the organization audit log API, which logs all security-relevant events across the organization.
